title: Conic Discriminant PASS: three directions straddling the parabola boundary
_fixture_purpose: Anchor fixture. Two hyperbola directions (I>0) and two ellipse directions (I<0) bracketing the parabola boundary. The fundamental direction (2,1) is the smallest primitive hyperbola (I=1). Direction (5,2) is the smallest primitive ellipse (I=-1). Together they show the boundary is between d/e≈2 and d/e≈2.5 — the silver ratio 1+√2≈2.414. Parabola impossibility: I=0 requires d²-e²=2de → (d/e)=1+√2, which is irrational (discriminant of x²-2x-1=0 equals 8, not a perfect square).

parabola_impossibility.conclusion: d/e is irrational → no primitive integer direction (d,e) satisfies I=0
parabola_impossibility.proof: If d,e∈Z, gcd(d,e)=1 and d²=e²+2de=e(e+2d), then since gcd(d,e)=1 we need e|d... but d²-2de-e²=0 has no integer solutions: the equation mod 4 gives d²-e²≡0(mod 4) i.e. (d-e)(d+e)≡0(mod 4); but also 2de≡0(mod 4) so de≡0(mod 2) — contradicts d-e odd for primitive direction.

directions._note: Fundamental QA direction; closest primitive integer hyperbola to parabola boundary
Smallest primitive ellipse (I=-1); closest to parabola boundary from the elliptic side

threshold.rule: d/e < 1+√2 → hyperbola; d/e = 1+√2 → parabola (impossible for integers); d/e > 1+√2 → ellipse
threshold.integer_approximants: Continued fraction [2;2,2,2,...] → convergents 2/1, 5/2, 12/5, 29/12, 70/29 — alternate between hyperbola and ellipse, approaching parabola

validation_checks.description: I=C-F correctly computed for all directions
